selected_direction.description: Stash 2.0 — A thought translator that distills fragmented AI prompts into time-compressed intent narratives, presented through an ambient pixel-art desktop companion
selected_direction.differentiator: The only product combining auto-distillation of AI coding prompts into layered intent narratives (narrative→summary→label) with an ambient desktop pet UI for peripheral awareness
selected_direction.rationale: User provided complete product spec; direction is user-defined, not explored
pre_mortem: Linux transparent/click-through window behavior varies across window managers (X11 vs Wayland, GNOME vs KDE)
Local 7-8B LLM models may produce poor Chinese intent distillation quality
Jump-to-terminal feature depends on terminal emulator-specific APIs

design_philosophy.justification: Default state uses only peripheral visual channel. Color and shape micro-changes convey status without consuming focal attention. Users should not feel like they are 'using' a tool.
Capture is completely invisible — monitors AI conversations with zero user input. Retrieval is meaningful — auto-adjusts detail granularity by time distance, like human memory.
Information flows from Stash to user. The only user input is occasional intent correction. Each context switch costs: glance 3s + click once. No more.
The only scenario where the pet may attract attention: a context status changed (done/stuck). Otherwise it stays quiet. No popups, no sounds, no proactive display.

stack: LLM hybrid: Ollama local (realtime) + Cloud API (compression)
tech_direction.product_impact: Always-on desktop companion with <50MB memory, <1% idle CPU, ~3-5s end-to-end latency for distillation
tech_direction.rationale: Tauri chosen over Electron for resource efficiency (10MB bundle vs 100MB+, <50MB RAM vs 200MB+). SQLite sufficient for ~10K records/year. Hybrid LLM balances speed and quality.

entities.name: Context
entities.description: A thing the developer is focused on. Usually maps to a project directory but not strictly — same project can have multiple contexts. Max 4 active.

entities.name: Intent
entities.description: Stash's understanding of what the developer is thinking in a context. Auto-distilled from prompts, stored in 3 tiers: narrative (2-4h), summary (1-3d), label (1w+). Forms an evolution chain.

entities.name: RawPrompt
entities.description: Raw captured data from AI conversations (Claude Code JSONL), git signals, or manual input. The source material for intent distillation.

entities.name: Status
entities.description: Runtime state of a context: running (active), done (awaiting review), stuck (blocked), parked (completed/shelved). Auto-detected from git signals, manually overridable.

relationships.description: Each context has an evolution chain of intents over time
Each context accumulates raw prompts from AI sessions
3-5 raw prompts are distilled into one narrative intent
Compressed intents reference their source intents via compressed_from
Each context has exactly one current status

non_functional_requirements.description: End-to-end latency from prompt capture to UI update
Disk usage for one year of data

non_functional_requirements.target: No content sanitization; user's LLM mode selection constitutes informed consent

mvp_features.name: F1 · Prompt Capture
mvp_features.feasibility: High — Claude Code JSONL format is stable and well-documented. Rust notify crate is mature.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: WakaTime tracks time only, not semantic content. No competitor captures prompt text.

mvp_features.name: F2 · Realtime Distillation
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — LLM quality for Chinese intent summarization needs validation. Sliding window trigger is straightforward.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Claude memory does per-conversation summary, not cross-session intent distillation. Echoes is retroactive/manual.

mvp_features.name: F3 · Periodic Compression
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — Timer-based, batch LLM calls. Compressed_from chain management adds complexity.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor has time-distance-based narrative compression.

mvp_features.name: F4 · Direction Change Detection
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — Semantic comparison via LLM. Can be combined with distillation call to reduce overhead.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Unique capability — no competitor detects thinking pivots.

mvp_features.name: F5 · Manual Correction
mvp_features.feasibility: High — Standard inline edit UI + new intent record.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: N/A — unique to Stash's intent model.

mvp_features.name: F6 · Context Auto-detection
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — Claude Code project hash → directory mapping needs reverse engineering.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor auto-detects project contexts from AI activity.

mvp_features.name: F7 · Status Auto-awareness
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — Git signal heuristics (commit timing, CI status) require tuning.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Partial overlap with CI dashboards, but those require browser/dashboard interaction.

mvp_features.name: F8 · Bubble Status Encoding
mvp_features.feasibility: Medium — Color/animation system in pixel art style. Ring arrangement algorithm for 1-4 bubbles.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Desktop pets exist (Shimeji, DesktopPet.app) but none encode real work state.

mvp_features.name: F9 · One-click Jump
mvp_features.feasibility: Low-Medium — Terminal focusing is terminal-emulator-specific on Linux. PR detection requires git remote parsing.
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Unique — no ambient tool provides context-aware workspace jumping.

context_dependencies: Claude Code JSONL session format stability (~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl)
Linux X11 window manager support for transparent + always-on-top + click-through
Ollama availability for local LLM inference
Git CLI availability on system PATH

selectedPages.name: card-popup
selectedPages.description: Muted Cyber arcade card — soft lavender + teal palette, pixel font headers, mono body

selectedPages.name: intent-graph
selectedPages.description: Two-column timeline — timestamps left, content right, Noto Serif editorial, condensed pill tags

selectedPages.name: pet-window
selectedPages.description: Speech bubble style status indicators around pixel pet sprite

selectedPages.name: manual-input
selectedPages.description: Global hotkey input dialog for manual intent (not yet designed)

selectedPages.name: settings
selectedPages.description: LLM config, pet position, hotkeys (not yet designed)
